2605.00315.title: Unbox Responsible GeoAI: Navigating Climate Extreme and Disaster Mapping
2605.00315.paper_summary: As climate extreme and disaster events become more frequent and intense, Geospatial Artificial Intelligence (GeoAI) has emerged as a transformative approach for large-scale disaster mapping and risk reduction. However, the purely mechanical, performance-driven deployment of GeoAI models can result in amplifying inherent spatial inequalities, preventing effective emergency decision-making, and producing severe environmental carbon footprint. To unbox the concept of responsible GeoAI, this position paper examines its emerging role, e.g., in climate extreme and disaster mapping, from a critical GIS perspective. We address the nexus of responsible GeoAI into four interrelated theoretical dimensions, specifically Representativeness, Explainability, Sustainability, and Ethics, with examples from climate extreme and disaster mapping. Moreover, targeting at the operational practice, we then propose a conceptual governance Model of responsible GeoAI that categorizes its governance practices into Data, Application, and Society scopes. Last, this position paper aims to raise the attention in the broader GIS community that the future of climate resilience relies not just on building better algorithms, but on fostering a governance ecosystem where GeoAI is deployed responsibly, ethically, and sustainably.
2605.00315.paper_summary_zh: 隨著氣候極端事件和災難事件變得越來越頻繁和劇烈，地理空間人工智慧（GeoAI）已成為大規模災難映射和風險減少的變革性方法。<lb/>然而，純粹機械化、以性能為驅動的GeoAI模型部署可能會加劇固有的空間不平等，阻礙有效的緊急決策，並產生嚴重的環境碳足跡。<lb/>為了解開負責任的GeoAI概念，這篇立場文件從批判性地理資訊系統的角度檢視其在氣候極端和災難映射中的新興角色。<lb/>我們將負責任的GeoAI的關聯性分為四個相互關聯的理論維度，具體包括代表性、可解釋性、可持續性和倫理，並舉出氣候極端和災難映射的例子。<lb/>此外，針對操作實踐，我們提出了一個負責任的GeoAI概念治理模型，將其治理實踐分為數據、應用和社會範疇。<lb/>最後，這篇立場文件旨在引起更廣泛的地理資訊系統社群的關注，未來的氣候韌性不僅依賴於建立更好的算法，還依賴於促進一個負責任、倫理和可持續的GeoAI部署治理生態系統。
2605.00315.author: Hao Li et.al.
